Package nonprofitbookkeeping.service

Class ChartOfAccountsIOService

java.lang.Object
nonprofitbookkeeping.service.ChartOfAccountsIOService
public final class ChartOfAccountsIOService extends Object
Utility for persisting a ChartOfAccounts to / from either a human-readable JSON document or an Excel spreadsheet (.xlsx). The JSON file format is: 

 {
   "_schemaVersion" : 1,
   "rootAccounts"   : [ { ... }, { ... } ]
 }

The mapper is pre-configured to:

  • write ISO-8601 strings for any java.time values,
  • indent output for readability,
  • ignore unknown JSON properties on import (forward compatibility).
This service uses Jackson for JSON processing.

  • Constructor Details

    • ChartOfAccountsIOService

      public ChartOfAccountsIOService()
      Constructs a new ChartOfAccountsIOService. Initializes and configures a Jackson ObjectMapper with settings for handling Java Time (JSR-310) types, disabling timestamp writing for dates (uses ISO-8601 strings), and enabling indented (pretty-printed) JSON output.

  • Method Details

    • exportToJson

      public void exportToJson(ChartOfAccounts coa, Path path) throws IOException
      Writes the given ChartOfAccounts object to a JSON file at the specified Path. If the parent directories for the path do not exist, they will be created. The output JSON will be pretty-printed.
      Parameters:
      coa - The ChartOfAccounts object to persist. Must not be null.
      path - The Path to the target JSON file. This can be an absolute or relative path. The file will be overwritten if it already exists.
      Throws:
      IOException - if an error occurs during directory creation or file writing, or if coa cannot be serialized to JSON.
      NullPointerException - if coa or path is null.

    • importFromJson

      public ChartOfAccounts importFromJson(Path path) throws IOException
      Reads a ChartOfAccounts object from the JSON file at the specified Path. The JSON file is expected to be in the format previously produced by exportToJson(ChartOfAccounts, Path).
      Parameters:
      path - The Path to the JSON file to read.
      Returns:
      A fully populated, mutable ChartOfAccounts object.
      Throws:
      IOException - if the file does not exist, cannot be read, or contains invalid JSON that cannot be deserialized into a ChartOfAccounts object.
      NullPointerException - if path is null.

    • exportToXlsx

      public static void exportToXlsx(ChartOfAccounts coa, Path path) throws IOException
      Writes the given ChartOfAccounts object to an Excel workbook at the specified Path. The workbook will contain a single sheet named "COA" with columns: Number, Name, Type, Parent, IncreaseSide, OpeningBalance, SupplementalKinds.
      Parameters:
      coa - The ChartOfAccounts to export. Must not be null.
      path - The destination Path for the workbook. Parent directories will be created if necessary.
      Throws:
      IOException - if an error occurs while writing the file.

    • importFromXlsx

      public static ChartOfAccounts importFromXlsx(Path path) throws IOException
      Reads a ChartOfAccounts from an Excel workbook at the given path. The expected sheet layout matches that produced by exportToXlsx(nonprofitbookkeeping.model.ChartOfAccounts, java.nio.file.Path).
      Parameters:
      path - The path to the workbook to read.
      Returns:
      A populated ChartOfAccounts instance.
      Throws:
      IOException - if the file cannot be read or parsed.
